Domain verification: https://www.osha.gov/ is the official OSHA website, https://www.epa.gov/ is the official EPA website. Both domains host downloadable PDF publications on the required topics.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires downloading real government safety documents from official US agencies: OSHA (osha.gov) and EPA (epa.gov). OSHA hosts PDF publications on fall protection, electrical safety, lockout/tagout, personal protective equipment (PPE), and hazard communication standards. EPA hosts PDF publications on indoor air quality, lead-based paint renovation (RRP Rule), refrigerant management (Section 608), and asbestos safety. The task requires minimum 2 OSHA documents and 1 EPA document. Documents are organized in ~/Documents/SafetyLibrary/ with OSHA/ and EPA/ subdirectories. A master index file (index.txt) documents each downloaded publication including filename, source URL, category, and brief description. Browser history verification confirms visits to osha.gov and epa.gov domains.
